title: Bloom Energy Q1 2026: Revenue Surges 130%, Guidance Raised
text: Bloom Energy reported Q1 2026 results with record revenue and raised full-year guidance. Revenue reached $751.05 million, up 130.4% from prior year. The company demonstrated strong operational improvements with non-GAAP EPS of $0.44, exceeding analyst expectations of $0.10 by 340%. Product revenue surged 208% year-over-year to $653.3 million. Gross margin expanded to 31.5%, up 280 basis points annually. Operating income surged to $129.7 million from $13.2 million in the prior year quarter. The company raised 2026 revenue guidance to $3.4-$3.8 billion, representing approximately 80% year-over-year growth at midpoint, up from prior guidance of approximately 60%. Non-GAAP gross margin guidance is approximately 34%. Non-GAAP operating income guidance is $600-$750 million. Non-GAAP EPS guidance is $1.85-$2.25. The company's backlog reached approximately $20 billion, with $6 billion in product orders and $14 billion in service contracts. Management's commentary emphasized rapid scaling capacity in hundreds of megawatts per quarter and continued execution on large-scale customer agreements.
